LHX.sentence: L3Harris is up slightly as the rocket motor business keeps building momentum alongside its bigger defense peers.

ASML.sentence: ASML is climbing again as they accelerate deliveries of their most advanced chipmaking machines to Taiwan's TSMC for the next generation of processors.

LIN.sentence: Linde is down a bit — the industrial gas business is steady but their profit margins have compressed from the unusually high levels of recent years.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft jumped nearly 3% as Azure cloud revenue keeps growing at over 30%, driven by companies paying big money to rent AI computing power.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher popped over 5% and is starting to look cheaper after a long slide — lab equipment demand is steady and the stock has fallen enough to enter bargain territory.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is flat but the dividend yield has gotten high enough that the stock looks a bit expensive compared to government bonds.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow surged over 5% even though it's still down nearly half from its peak — enterprise customers keep signing up for AI workflow tools despite the stock's rough year.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore is down slightly and lagging behind copper prices, which is unusual since they're one of the world's biggest copper miners.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin dropped about 3% but is still well above its long-term trend line, so the pullback looks healthy after the big run-up.

KOF.sentence: Coca-Cola FEMSA is down slightly as the Mexican peso stays weak against the dollar, which hurts when you convert profits back.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras is flat — oil prices are staying well above what they need to make money, so the dividend machine keeps humming.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk is up slightly but trading below the replacement cost of their ships, which suggests global shipping demand might be softer than it looks.
